evidence_quotes.quote: Many digital goods, like software and video games, are advertised as purchases, but in reality, users often only receive limited licenses that can be revoked at any time.
Subscription services often use tricky designs to make cancelling difficult.
Hidden charges, complicated steps, or requiring users to unsubscribe through different platforms are common obstacles.
Many games include paid chance-based features like "loot boxes", which closely resemble gambling and are often accessible to children.
Lastly, digital products are frequently discontinued without any plan for what happens afterward, leaving people unable to access content they paid for.

paragraphs.text: As a resident of the EU, I fully back the goals of the Digital Fairness Act and want to emphasize some important areas where stronger consumer protections are needed. Many digital goods, like software and video games, are advertised as purchases, but in reality, users often only receive limited licenses that can be revoked at any time. This can mislead buyers, especially when access is suddenly cut offeven for products that dont need online connections to work. Its important that the Act requires clear and honest information about what consumers are actually getting, along with better protections for digital ownership rights. Subscription services often use tricky designs to make cancelling difficult. Hidden charges, complicated steps, or requiring users to unsubscribe through different platforms are common obstacles. These practices should be banned, and cancelling a subscription should be as easy as signing up. Many games include paid chance-based features like "loot boxes", which closely resemble gambling and are often accessible to children. These should be properly regulated, with clear warnings and age restrictions. Lastly, digital products are frequently discontinued without any plan for what happens afterward, leaving people unable to access content they paid for. Companies should, whenever possible, provide offline access or support community efforts to preserve these products. The Digital Fairness Act offers a crucial chance to ensure consumers are treated fairly in todays digital world. I encourage the Commission to tackle these challenges and protect the rights of digital users throughout Europe.
